message:
fix(ingest): emit GAP-only segment for explicit upstream gap markers instead of fabricating empty deltas

When the mux subscription delivers an explicit gap (overflow=true with
empty data), the StreamIngester was previously emitting both a GAP segment
and a zero-length Delta segment. The empty delta is meaningless and pollutes
the captured segment stream, confusing downstream consumers that expect
every Delta to carry actual content.

Changes:
- ingest.rs: Add early return for empty non-overflow events (no-op).
  After emitting the GAP for an overflow event with empty data, return
  immediately instead of falling through to the delta capture path.
  Adds doc comment on the `data` field explaining the empty-data convention.
  Adds test `ingester_empty_overflow_event_emits_gap_without_empty_delta`
  verifying the full sequence: normal delta → empty overflow → GAP only →
  next delta resumes with correct sequence numbering.

- tailer.rs: Update StreamingBridge doc comments and inline comments to
  reflect the new contract (overflow + empty data = GAP-only output).
  Fix test `streaming_bridge_process_gap_emits_gap_segment` to assert
  exactly one GAP segment with empty content, matching the corrected behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
